policy.title: RBI Issues Final Guidelines on Climate Risk Management for Scheduled Commercial Banks
policy.description: RBI mandates all scheduled commercial banks to integrate climate scenario analysis into ICAAP by March 2027, with transition risk stress testing required for exposures exceeding ₹100 crore. Banks must establish dedicated climate risk units and report physical risk exposures by geography and sector quarterly.

policy.title: SEBI Expands BRSR Core to Top 500 Listed Companies with Enhanced Climate Metrics
policy.description: SEBI extends mandatory BRSR Core reporting to top 500 companies by market cap effective FY2026-27, adding Scope 3 emissions disclosure and water stress metrics. Third-party assurance now required for all nine BRSR Core KPIs, creating demand for verified climate data.
policy.source: Securities and Exchange Board of India

policy.title: IRDAI Releases Sandbox Guidelines for Parametric Climate Insurance Products
policy.description: IRDAI opens regulatory sandbox for parametric insurance products covering flood, drought, and cyclone perils, allowing 24-month pilot periods with relaxed premium pricing norms. Insurers must demonstrate trigger-data integrity through third-party validation and IoT-based verification.
policy.source: Insurance Regulatory and Development Authority of India

policy.title: Basel Committee Finalizes Pillar 3 Climate Disclosure Requirements
policy.description: Basel Committee issues final Pillar 3 climate disclosure framework requiring banks to report financed emissions, physical risk concentrations, and transition pathway alignment from January 2027. Standardized templates mandate granular sector-geography breakdowns for credit exposures.

policy.title: ISSB Announces Emerging Market Implementation Guidance for IFRS S2
policy.description: ISSB releases jurisdiction-specific guidance for emerging markets implementing IFRS S2 climate disclosures, including proportionality provisions for Scope 3 and scenario analysis. India identified as priority jurisdiction with MCA consultation expected by Q3 2026.

policy.title: NGFS Releases Phase V Climate Scenarios with Enhanced Physical Risk Granularity
policy.description: NGFS publishes updated scenarios with sub-national physical risk projections for 180 countries, including district-level India data for floods, heat stress, and water scarcity. New acute risk modules enable bank-level portfolio stress testing against 1-in-100 year events.
policy.source: Network for Greening the Financial System

competitors.title: Jupiter Intelligence Raises $110M Series D, Launches Bank-Specific Climate PD Module
competitors.description: Jupiter closed $110M Series D led by TPG Rise Climate to expand into credit risk integration, launching ClimateScore Financial with PD adjustment factors for 12 physical hazards. India expansion announced with Mumbai office opening in Q2 2026.

competitors.title: Sprih Partners with HDFC Bank for Enterprise-Wide ESG Data Management
competitors.description: Sprih announced strategic partnership with HDFC Bank to deploy its ESG platform across 6,000+ branches for Scope 1-3 tracking and BRSR reporting. Deal reportedly valued at ₹45 crore over three years with exclusivity for retail banking sector.

competitors.title: Moody's Launches Physical Risk-Adjusted LGD Model for Emerging Market Portfolios
competitors.description: Moody's released Climate LGD Adjust covering 45 emerging markets with property-level physical risk integration into loss-given-default estimates. Model covers flood, cyclone, and wildfire perils with claimed 90% accuracy on historical loss correlation.

competitors.title: XDI Partners with Yes Bank for Physical Risk Assessment of Commercial Lending Portfolio
competitors.description: XDI signed agreement with Yes Bank to assess physical climate risk across ₹1.2 lakh crore commercial real estate and infrastructure portfolio. Pilot covers 15,000 assets across 200 districts using XDI's GLOBE platform.

competitors.title: CarbonMint Raises $8M Series A for Agricultural Carbon Credit Platform
competitors.description: CarbonMint secured $8M Series A from Omnivore and Accel to scale its blockchain-based agricultural carbon credit verification platform. Company targets 500,000 farmer onboarding by 2027 with parametric insurance bundling as new revenue stream.

competitors.title: Watershed Launches Financial Services Module with Portfolio Emissions Attribution
competitors.description: Watershed released dedicated financial services product enabling PCAF-aligned portfolio emissions calculation with automated data collection from 50+ data providers. Module includes transition risk scoring and ISSB-ready reporting templates.

research.title: Climate Risk and Bank Capital: Evidence from Indian Commercial Banks
research.description: RBI study finds Indian banks face potential 8-12% increase in credit risk-weighted assets under severe physical risk scenarios, with agriculture and MSME portfolios most exposed. Coastal state exposures show 2.3x higher expected loss variance than national average.

research.title: Parametric Insurance for Climate Resilience: Scaling Solutions in South Asia
research.description: World Bank analysis estimates $4.2 billion annual parametric insurance opportunity in India across agriculture, infrastructure, and supply chain segments by 2030. Report identifies basis risk reduction and regulatory clarity as primary adoption barriers.

research.title: Physical Climate Risk in Credit Risk Modelling: A Framework for Banks
research.description: UNEPFI framework provides standardized methodology for integrating physical hazard data into PD and LGD models, with sector-specific adjustment factors for 18 industries. Pilot with 12 global banks showed 15-40% variation in risk estimates versus traditional models.

research.title: ESG Data Availability and Quality in Emerging Markets
research.description: IFC assessment reveals only 23% of listed companies in emerging markets provide climate disclosures meeting ISSB minimum standards, with India at 31% versus 18% Sub-Saharan Africa average. Report identifies physical risk exposure data as largest gap across all regions.

research.title: Sigma Report: Climate Physical Risk and Indian Insurance Markets
research.description: Swiss Re estimates India's annual average insured losses from climate perils will reach $8.5 billion by 2030, up from $3.2 billion in 2024. Flood and cyclone concentration in uninsured coastal urban areas identified as systemic risk for banking sector.

research.title: Index-Based Insurance for Financial Institutions: Design and Distribution Considerations
research.description: Munich Re technical paper outlines design parameters for bank-distributed parametric products, recommending minimum 0.85 correlation between index and actual loss for retail products. Analysis shows banks can reduce basis risk by 40% through portfolio-level triggers versus individual coverage.

ourRead.headline: RBI's March 2027 deadline just created urgency—the question is whether StepChange can outpace Jupiter and XDI's sudden India pivot.
body: This month's signals converge on a single theme: regulatory crystallization is pulling climate risk from 'nice-to-have' to 'must-have' for Indian banks, and global players have noticed. The RBI's final guidelines, SEBI's BRSR expansion, and IRDAI's parametric sandbox create a synchronized compliance wave that will drive procurement decisions in the next 12-18 months. For StepChange, this is both validation and a starting gun.

The RBI guidelines (policy-1) are the most consequential development. The March 2027 ICAAP integration mandate with ₹100 crore exposure thresholds means every large bank needs district-level physical risk data mapped to credit portfolios within 12 months. Critically, the requirement for dedicated climate risk units creates internal champions who will need external data partners. StepChange should immediately prepare RBI-aligned pitch materials showing how our hazard data directly feeds into the mandated scenario analysis framework—compliance specificity beats generic climate risk messaging.

The competitive landscape just got more aggressive. Jupiter's $110M raise and explicit India expansion (comp-1) combined with XDI's Yes Bank deal (comp-4) signal that global players view India as the next battleground. However, Sprih's HDFC partnership (comp-2) shows Indian incumbents are winning on relationship depth rather than technical sophistication. StepChange's advantage lies in the intersection: we can offer global-caliber physical risk models with India-specific granularity that Jupiter lacks and credit risk integration that Sprih cannot provide. The XDI-Yes Bank pilot is the immediate competitive threat—we should target 2-3 PSU banks to establish comparable reference cases before Jupiter's Mumbai office opens.

The UNEPFI framework (research-3) and RBI's own study (research-1) provide intellectual ammunition StepChange should weaponize. The 8-12% RWA impact finding gives CFOs a number they can take to their boards, while UNEPFI's sector adjustment factors validate our PD/LGD modeling approach. We should publish a technical note showing how StepChange's methodology aligns with both frameworks—thought leadership now translates to vendor shortlists in Q4.

Watch IRDAI's sandbox approvals closely—CarbonMint's parametric insurance pivot (comp-5) signals agri-fintech convergence we should monitor. Action item: schedule workshops with 5 target banks in April specifically framed around 'RBI March 2027 readiness' while the guidelines are fresh and budgets are being allocated.
